methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Matic Robotics is a seed-stage consumer tech company founded by Navneet Dalal and Mehul Nariyawala that has raised $107M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a limited peer universe of one private comparable (Vital Lyfe) and no public market references in the consumer tech sector.

Stage-progression methodology was the applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total capital raised ($107M) as a proxy for enterprise value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to the subsequent financing round yields an implied valuation of $25M. This reflects a conservative interpretation in which the $107M raised represents cumulative capital deployed rather than a direct valuation anchor, with the step-up capturing anticipated value accretion before Series A.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point estimate of $25M. This range incorporates 15% downside and 15% upside variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of public comparables, the single private peer reference point, and limited visibility into unit economics or market traction metrics. The narrow comp universe and reliance on a single methodology warrant heightened caution; additional revenue, customer, or product-stage data would substantially improve conviction in any point estimate within or beyond this range.
